component_identification.content: Base game includes: 1 central board, 4 player boards, villager tokens in player colors, house/outpost/cave cards and tiles, goods tokens (fish, cider, mushrooms, etc.), coins, reputation markers, first-player marker, key/location tiles, encounter book, and rulebook.

core_game_loop.content: Players take turns assigning villagers to village actions (harvest, build, recruit, explore, train). Exploring triggers narrative encounters read from the encounter book where choices require skill checks and may reward resources, caves, or setbacks. After all pass, perform end-of-round upkeep/income and begin a new round. Game lasts 7 rounds.

detailed_rules.content: Villagers can be exhausted or ready; some actions require multiple villagers or specific icons. Building structures expands action options and income. Exploration sends a team below ground: reader presents encounter, active player chooses options, then rolls custom dice plus lantern/sword/scroll skills from assigned villagers/buildings. Success/failure branches to different outcomes including cave cards, reputation shifts, resources, injuries, or immediate points. Reputation affects final scoring and some card interactions; balancing productive villagers vs explorers is central tension. Coins can substitute in limited contexts per card text, but not universally. Key rule edge cases come from encounter instructions; encounter text overrides generic rules when conflict occurs.

scoring_and_endgame.content: After round 7, score points from buildings, caves/outposts, reputation track, and specific card effects. Add remaining coins/resources converted as instructed by rules or cards. Highest total wins; tie-breaker is highest reputation, then most coins.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Open with income-producing buildings so exploration has economic support. 2) Don’t overcommit weak explorers; failed checks can be brutally inefficient. 3) Build villager skill diversity to improve encounter options. 4) Reputation is not optional—many end points hinge on it. 5) Time final-round actions for direct VP rather than long-payback engines.
